attacking_style: Explosive attacks on exceptionally steep gradients (14%+), often from already-formed breakaways rather than the peloton. Thrives in short, punchy summit finishes (3km walls) where gradient spikes.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with significant autonomy, often infiltrating breakaways early. Limited evidence of coordinated team support; potential internal hierarchy issues when multiple team leaders present.
terrain_preference: Excels on steep-percentage climbs (14%+) and short, brutal summit finishes. Strong in chaotic race situations where positioning matters more than sustained tempo. Less effective on longer, grinding ascents.
vulnerability: Struggles when team tactics are unclear or when teammates are also in contention. May lack the sustained power for longer climbs where pure watts-per-kilo matters more than punch.
key_patterns: Operates stealthily without visible attacks; GC gains achieved through positioning not spectacle (2 instances)
Exceptional acceleration on 14%+ gradients; drops competitors immediately when gradient spikes (2 instances)
Works from breakaways rather than peloton; bridges then attacks from established groups (2 instances)
Form inconsistency across seasons; dropped by teammate in 2019 after winning solo in 2018
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 showed podium GC result but no attacking flair; unclear if conserving energy tactically or lacking explosive form from 2018 peak
